claim.exact_text: Electric vehicles produce no carbon emissions.
claim.normalized_text: Electric vehicles produce no carbon emissions.

evidence.title: Electric Vehicle Myths
evidence.excerpt: Electric vehicles typically have a smaller carbon footprint than gasoline cars, even when accounting for charging.

evidence.title: Emissions from Electric Vehicles
evidence.excerpt: All-electric vehicles produce zero direct emissions.

evidence.title: Life-cycle greenhouse gas emissions
evidence.excerpt: Life-cycle assessment includes emissions from vehicle and battery production and energy use.

verdict.label: Misleading
verdict.explanation: Electric vehicles have no tailpipe emissions. Manufacturing and electricity generation can still produce lifecycle emissions.
verdict.uncertainty: The total varies with battery production and the electricity mix.
verdict.counterevidence_summary: Zero direct emissions does not mean zero lifecycle emissions.
verdict.common_ground: Electric vehicles eliminate tailpipe emissions and generally reduce lifecycle emissions.
